content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (like ICM and RND from autonomous agent research) can be repurposed to prioritize which new unlabeled data samples get stored in an active learning agent’s long-term memory, cutting redundant storage and boosting out-of-distribution generalization for 2024–2025 SOTA memory architectures. Instead of pre-defining memory pruning rules, this system uses the agent’s own inherent curiosity signal to automatically retain only the samples that expand its current knowledge frontier.
